words.definitionEn: having enough skill or knowledge to do something well or to the necessary standard
words.exampleEn: He's very competent in his work.

words.definitionEn: to produce a book, list, report, etc. by bringing together different items, articles, songs, etc.; to collect information in order to produce a book, list, etc.
words.exampleEn: We are trying to compile a list of suitable people for the job.

words.definitionEn: to add to something in a way that improves it or makes it more attractive
words.exampleEn: The excellent menu is complemented by a good wine list.

words.definitionEn: a young person who is developing from a child into an adult
words.exampleEn: adolescents between the ages of 13 and 18 and the problems they face

words.definitionEn: the state of being formed of many parts; the state of being difficult to understand
words.exampleEn: the increasing complexity of modern telecommunication systems

words.definitionEn: the practice of obeying rules or requests made by people in authority
words.exampleEn: compliance (with something), procedures that must be followed to ensure full compliance with the law

words.definitionEn: a thing that makes a situation more complicated or difficult
words.exampleEn: The bad weather added a further complication to our journey.

words.definitionEn: to obey a rule, an order, etc.; to meet particular standards
words.exampleEn: They refused to comply with the UN resolution.

words.definitionEn: the different parts that something is made of; the way in which the different parts are organized
words.exampleEn: the chemical composition of the soil

words.definitionEn: an agreement made between two people or groups in which each side gives up some of the things they want so that both sides are happy at the end
words.exampleEn: After lengthy talks the two sides finally reached a compromise.

words.definitionEn: to give up some of your demands in order to reach an agreement after disagreeing with somebody
words.exampleEn: Neither side is prepared to compromise.

words.definitionEn: to calculate something
words.exampleEn: The losses were computed at £5 million.

words.definitionEn: the act of adopting a child; the fact of being adopted
words.exampleEn: She put the baby up for adoption.

words.definitionEn: to hide somebody/something
words.exampleEn: conceal somebody/something, The paintings were concealed beneath a thick layer of plaster.

words.definitionEn: to admit that something is true, logical, etc. after first denying it or resisting it
words.exampleEn: + speech, ‘Not bad,’ she conceded grudgingly.

words.definitionEn: to form an idea, a plan, etc. in your mind
words.exampleEn: He conceived the idea of transforming the old power station into an arts centre.

words.definitionEn: an understanding or a belief of what something/somebody is or what something/somebody should be
words.exampleEn: conception of something, Marx’s conception of social justice

words.definitionEn: something that you allow or do, or allow somebody to have, in order to end an argument or to make a situation less difficult
words.exampleEn: The firm will be forced to make concessions if it wants to avoid a strike.

words.definitionEn: to say very strongly that you think something is bad, usually for moral reasons
words.exampleEn: condemn somebody/something, The government issued a statement condemning the killings.

words.definitionEn: to discuss something with somebody, in order to exchange opinions or get advice
words.exampleEn: He wanted to confer with his colleagues before reaching a decision.

words.definitionEn: a statement that a person makes, admitting that they are guilty of a crime; the act of making such a statement
words.exampleEn: After hours of questioning by police, she made a full confession.

words.definitionEn: an arrangement of the parts of something or a group of things; the form or shape that this arrangement produces
words.exampleEn: configuration of something, The design is based on four configurations of squares.

words.definitionEn: to keep somebody/something inside the limits of a particular activity, subject, area, etc.
words.exampleEn: be confined to (doing) something, The work will not be confined to the Glasgow area.

words.definitionEn: a statement, letter, etc. that shows that something is true, correct or definite
words.exampleEn: I'm still waiting for confirmation of the test results.

words.definitionEn: to appear and need to be dealt with by somebody
words.exampleEn: What is to be done about the economic problems confronting the country?

words.definitionEn: a situation in which there is anger between people or groups who disagree because they have different opinions
words.exampleEn: confrontation (with somebody), She wanted to avoid another confrontation with her father.

words.definitionEn: to tell somebody that you are pleased about their success or achievements
words.exampleEn: I congratulated them all on their results.

words.definitionEn: a group of people who are gathered together in a church for a religious service, not including the priest and choir
words.exampleEn: The congregation stood to sing the hymn.

words.definitionEn: related to or belonging to a congress or the Congress in the US
words.exampleEn: a congressional committee/bill

words.definitionEn: to take control of a country or city and its people by force
words.exampleEn: The Normans conquered England in 1066.

words.definitionEn: the part of your mind that tells you whether your actions are right or wrong
words.exampleEn: to have a clear/guilty conscience (= to feel that you have done right/wrong)

words.definitionEn: the state of being able to use your senses and mental powers to understand what is happening
words.exampleEn: I can't remember any more—I must have lost consciousness.

words.definitionEn: following one after another in a continuous series
words.exampleEn: She was absent for nine consecutive days.

words.definitionEn: an opinion that all members of a group agree with
words.exampleEn: consensus (about/on something), She is skilled at achieving consensus on sensitive issues.

words.definitionEn: permission to do something, especially given by somebody in authority
words.exampleEn: The written consent of a parent is required.

words.definitionEn: to agree to something or give your permission for something
words.exampleEn: When she told them what she intended, they readily consented.

words.definitionEn: to protect something and prevent it from being changed or destroyed
words.exampleEn: new laws to conserve wildlife in the area

words.definitionEn: the quality of always behaving in the same way or of having the same opinions, standard, etc.; the quality of being consistent
words.exampleEn: She has played with great consistency all season.

words.definitionEn: to make a position of power or success stronger so that it is more likely to continue
words.exampleEn: With this new movie he has consolidated his position as the country's leading director.

words.definitionEn: the fact of somebody being away from a place where they are usually expected to be; the occasion or period of time when somebody is away
words.exampleEn: in somebody's absence, The decision was made in my absence (= while I was not there).

words.definitionEn: a district that elects its own representative to parliament
words.exampleEn: Unemployment is high in her constituency.

words.definitionEn: to be considered to be something
words.exampleEn: Does such an activity constitute a criminal offence?

words.definitionEn: the system of laws and basic principles that a state, a country or an organization is governed by
words.exampleEn: your right to vote under the constitution

words.definitionEn: a thing that limits something, or limits your freedom to do something
words.exampleEn: constraints of time/money/space

words.definitionEn: the act of discussing something with somebody or with a group of people before making a decision about it
words.exampleEn: a consultation document/paper/period/process

words.definitionEn: to think about whether you should do something, or how you should do something
words.exampleEn: contemplate something, You're too young to be contemplating retirement.

words.definitionEn: negative and unpleasant; not likely to produce a good result
words.exampleEn: adverse change/circumstances/weather conditions

words.definitionEn: the feeling that somebody/something is without value and deserves no respect at all
words.exampleEn: with contempt, She looked at him with contempt.

words.definitionEn: to say that something is true, especially in an argument
words.exampleEn: I would contend that the minister's thinking is flawed on this point.

words.definitionEn: a person or team with a chance of winning a competition
words.exampleEn: contender (for something), a contender for a gold medal in the Olympics

words.definitionEn: happy and satisfied with what you have
words.exampleEn: He seemed more content, less bitter.

words.definitionEn: anger between people who disagree
words.exampleEn: One area of contention is the availability of nursery care.

words.definitionEn: in a way that is repeated many times so that it is annoying
words.exampleEn: They argue continually about money.

words.definitionEn: a person or company that has a contract to do work or provide goods or services for another company
words.exampleEn: a building/roofing/electrical contractor

words.definitionEn: a lack of agreement between facts, opinions, actions, etc.
words.exampleEn: contradiction (between A and B), There is a contradiction between the two sets of figures.

words.definitionEn: the opposite fact, event or situation
words.exampleEn: In the end the contrary was proved true: he was innocent and she was guilty.

words.definitionEn: a person who writes articles for a magazine, book or website, or who talks on a radio or television programme or at a meeting
words.exampleEn: a regular contributor to this magazine

words.definitionEn: the act or process of changing something from one form, use or system to another
words.exampleEn: Their main business is the conversion of farm buildings into family homes.

words.definitionEn: to decide and state officially in court that somebody is guilty of a crime
words.exampleEn: be convicted (of something), He was convicted of fraud.

words.definitionEn: the act of finding somebody guilty of a crime in court; the fact of having been found guilty
words.exampleEn: He plans to appeal against his conviction.

words.definitionEn: to work together with somebody else in order to achieve something
words.exampleEn: The two groups agreed to cooperate with each other.

words.definitionEn: involving doing something together or working together with others towards a shared aim
words.exampleEn: Cooperative activity is essential to effective community work.

words.definitionEn: to organize the different parts of an activity and the people involved in it so that it works well
words.exampleEn: coordinate something, They appointed a new manager to coordinate the work of the team.

words.definitionEn: the act of making parts of something, groups of people, etc. work together in an efficient and organized way
words.exampleEn: The aim was to improve the coordination of services.

words.definitionEn: a person who organizes the different parts of an activity and the people involved in it so that it works well
words.exampleEn: The campaign needs an effective coordinator.

words.definitionEn: a police officer
words.exampleEn: Somebody call the cops!

words.definitionEn: a chemical element. Copper is a soft red-brown metal used for making electric wires, pipes and coins.
words.exampleEn: a copper mine

words.definitionEn: if a person or an organization holds the copyright on a piece of writing, music, etc., they are the only people who have the legal right to publish, broadcast, perform it, etc., and other people must ask their permission to use it or any part of it
words.exampleEn: Copyright expires seventy years after the death of the author.

words.definitionEn: a person who supports or speaks in favour of somebody or of a public plan or action
words.exampleEn: advocate for something/somebody, an advocate for hospital workers

words.definitionEn: a change that makes something more accurate than it was before
words.exampleEn: I've made a few small corrections to your report.

words.definitionEn: if two or more facts, figures, etc. correlate or if a fact, figure, etc. correlates with another, the facts are closely connected and affect or depend on each other
words.exampleEn: The figures do not seem to correlate.

words.definitionEn: a connection between two things in which one thing changes as the other does
words.exampleEn: correlation between A and B, There is a direct correlation between exposure to sun and skin cancer.

words.definitionEn: to be the same as or match something
words.exampleEn: Your account and hers do not correspond.

words.definitionEn: to support something publicly
words.exampleEn: advocate something, The group does not advocate the use of violence.

words.definitionEn: the letters, emails, etc. a person sends and receives
words.exampleEn: personal/private correspondence

words.definitionEn: a person who reports news from a particular country or on a particular subject for a newspaper or a television or radio station
words.exampleEn: She's the BBC's political correspondent.

words.definitionEn: matching or connected with something that you have just mentioned
words.exampleEn: A change in the money supply brings a corresponding change in expenditure.

words.definitionEn: willing to use their power to do dishonest or illegal things in return for money or to get an advantage
words.exampleEn: It was seen as the only way to overthrow a corrupt regime.

words.definitionEn: dishonest or illegal behaviour, especially of people in authority
words.exampleEn: allegations of bribery and corruption

words.definitionEn: costing a lot of money, especially more than you want to pay
words.exampleEn: Buying new furniture may prove too costly.

words.definitionEn: not in a place because of illness, etc.
words.exampleEn: He was absent from work for two weeks.

words.definitionEn: connected with beauty and art and the understanding of beautiful things
words.exampleEn: the aesthetic appeal of the songs

words.definitionEn: a member of a city or county council
words.exampleEn: Councillor Ann Jones

words.definitionEn: professional advice about a problem
words.exampleEn: The couple decided to go for relationship counselling.

words.definitionEn: a person who has been trained to advise people with problems, especially personal problems
words.exampleEn: I went to see a debt counsellor and she agreed to come to the bank with me.

words.definitionEn: to reply to somebody by trying to prove that what they said is not true
words.exampleEn: counter somebody/something, Such arguments are not easily countered.

words.definitionEn: a person or thing that has the same position or function as somebody/something else in a different place or situation
words.exampleEn: The Foreign Minister held talks with his Chinese counterpart.

words.definitionEn: very many; too many to be counted or mentioned
words.exampleEn: I've warned her countless times.

words.definitionEn: a sudden change of government that is illegal and often violent
words.exampleEn: He seized power in a military coup in 2008.

words.definitionEn: polite behaviour that shows respect for other people
words.exampleEn: I was treated with the utmost courtesy by the staff.

words.definitionEn: the feeling of liking or loving somebody/something very much and caring about them
words.exampleEn: Children need lots of love and affection.

words.definitionEn: to make something using special skills, especially with your hands
words.exampleEn: be crafted (from something), All the furniture is crafted from natural materials.

words.definitionEn: to move forward on your hands and knees or with your body close to the ground
words.exampleEn: Our baby is just starting to crawl.

words.definitionEn: a person who has made or invented a particular thing
words.exampleEn: Walt Disney, the creator of Mickey Mouse

words.definitionEn: the quality that somebody/something has that makes people believe or trust them
words.exampleEn: to gain/lack/lose credibility

words.definitionEn: that can be believed or trusted
words.exampleEn: a credible explanation/witness

words.definitionEn: to move slowly, quietly and carefully, because you do not want to be seen or heard
words.exampleEn: I crept up the stairs, trying not to wake my parents.

words.definitionEn: a piece of written criticism of a set of ideas, a work of art, etc.
words.exampleEn: She wrote a feminist critique of Freud's theories.

words.definitionEn: an object in the shape of a circle, usually made of gold and precious stones, that a king or queen wears on his or her head on official occasions
words.exampleEn: The crown was placed upon the new monarch's head.

words.definitionEn: in its natural state, before it has been processed or refined
words.exampleEn: crude oil/metal

words.definitionEn: to press something so hard that it is damaged or injured, or loses its shape
words.exampleEn: The car was completely crushed under the truck.

words.definitionEn: a small piece of a substance with many even sides, that is formed naturally when the substance becomes solid
words.exampleEn: ice/salt crystals

words.definitionEn: very popular with a particular group of people
words.exampleEn: a cult movie/book

words.definitionEn: a way of life, an attitude, an idea, etc. that has become very popular
words.exampleEn: the cult of physical fitness

words.definitionEn: to prepare and use land for growing plants or crops
words.exampleEn: The land around here has never been cultivated.

words.definitionEn: a strong desire to know about something
words.exampleEn: Children show curiosity about everything.

words.definitionEn: the legal right or duty to take care of or keep somebody/something; the act of taking care of something/somebody
words.exampleEn: Who will have custody of the children?

words.definitionEn: the situation that exists as a result of an important (and usually unpleasant) event, especially a war, an accident, etc.
words.exampleEn: in the aftermath of something, A lot of rebuilding took place in the aftermath of the war.

words.definitionEn: an article or a story that you cut from a newspaper or magazine and keep
words.exampleEn: newspaper/press cuttings

words.definitionEn: believing that people only do things to help themselves rather than for good or honest reasons
words.exampleEn: a cynical view/smile

words.definitionEn: a barrier that is built across a river in order to stop the water from flowing, used especially to make a reservoir (= a lake for storing water) or to produce electricity
words.exampleEn: the Narmada dam in India

words.definitionEn: causing damage; having a bad effect on somebody/something
words.exampleEn: damaging consequences/effects

words.definitionEn: the time of day when light first appears
words.exampleEn: at dawn, They start work at dawn.

words.definitionEn: pieces of wood, metal, building materials, etc. that are left after something has been destroyed
words.exampleEn: Emergency teams are still clearing the debris from the plane crash.

words.definitionEn: the first public appearance of a performer or sports player
words.exampleEn: He will make his debut for the first team this week.

words.definitionEn: the process of deciding about something important, especially in a group of people or in an organization
words.exampleEn: responsibility for decision-making

words.definitionEn: very important for the final result of a particular situation
words.exampleEn: a decisive factor/victory/battle

words.definitionEn: an official or formal statement, especially about the plans of a government or an organization; the act of making such a statement
words.exampleEn: to issue/sign a declaration

words.definitionEn: working hard at something because it is very important to you
words.exampleEn: a dedicated teacher

words.definitionEn: the hard work and effort that somebody puts into an activity or a purpose because they think it is important
words.exampleEn: hard work and dedication

words.definitionEn: a thing that somebody does that is usually very good or very bad
words.exampleEn: It's a stirring tale of heroic deeds.

words.definitionEn: to have a particular opinion about somebody/something
words.exampleEn: deem somebody/something + noun, The evening was deemed a great success.

words.definitionEn: what happens or appears if you do not make any other choice or change, especially in a computer program
words.exampleEn: The default is fifty lines.

words.definitionEn: a fault in something or in the way it has been made that means that it is not perfect
words.exampleEn: a defect in the glass

words.definitionEn: protecting somebody/something against attack
words.exampleEn: As a defensive measure he built a series of coastal forts and watchtowers.

words.definitionEn: the state of not having, or not having enough of, something that is essential
words.exampleEn: deficiency (in something), Vitamin deficiency in the diet can cause illness.

words.definitionEn: the amount by which money spent or owed is greater than money earned in a particular period of time
words.exampleEn: a budget/trade deficit

words.definitionEn: to refuse to obey or show respect for somebody in authority, a law, a rule, etc.
words.exampleEn: I wouldn't have dared to defy my teachers.

words.definitionEn: a person who is chosen or elected to represent the views of a group of people and vote and make decisions for them
words.exampleEn: Congress delegates rejected the proposals.

words.definitionEn: a group of people who represent the views of an organization, a country, etc.
words.exampleEn: the Dutch delegation to the United Nations

words.definitionEn: easily damaged or broken
words.exampleEn: delicate china teacups

words.definitionEn: an evil spirit
words.exampleEn: The people believed the girl was possessed by demons.

words.definitionEn: a statement that something is not true or does not exist; the action of denying something
words.exampleEn: denial (of something), the prisoner’s repeated denials of the charges against him
